behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three source clients -> aggregator/orchestrator -> summary step input). MOCK-MISS: this is not just one pure function; omission bugs appear at wiring boundaries. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP checks are negative for this behavior itself: we can stub source clients and assert the exact payload passed downstream. Rejected Unit because behavior text explicitly requires inclusion from all three sources (module interaction). Rejected System/Agentic because correctness here is payload assembly, not real network behavior or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregator/orchestrator modules with test doubles for the three data-source adapters that return distinct sentinel values. Invoke report generation up to the summary-call boundary and assert the payload passed to summary generation contains all three sentinel sections with correct mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/orchestration across modules: source adapter error propagation, section rendering, and pipeline continuation. A fake failing adapter reproduces the target failure without real network. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network semantics, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected Unit because fallback behavior spans adapter + orchestrator + renderer. Rejected System because behavior text targets graceful degradation logic, not transport-level timeout fidelity.
behaviors.test_description: Use integrated report pipeline modules with one source adapter configured to raise an availability error and the other two returning valid data. Assert run completes, report contains `data unavailable` for only the failed section, and remaining sections are present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the summary model's actual output quality (factuality/faithfulness to inputs). Deterministic stubs would hide model misrepresentation failures (deterministic-assertion trap). Single model decision boundary, not a multi-agent chain, so Agentic not Workflow. Rejected Integration because it can only validate routing/payload, not whether the real model preserves key values.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against the real model/API on a fixed dataset with known key values and acceptance checks for factual inclusion/exact numeric preservation (with tolerance rules). Execute multiple trials to capture non-deterministic drift and fail if faithfulness falls below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery/inbox behavior (SMTP/API transport, acceptance, delivery path). MOCK-HIDE: mocked email providers can pass while real delivery fails. This requires real HTTP/SMTP/network infrastructure, so System. Rejected Integration because inbox reachability/acceptance is outside in-process wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because pass criterion is end-to-end delivery, not model-decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end run in a staging environment using real source endpoints (or production-like test instances) and real email delivery service to a controlled mailbox. Assert message is received in inbox with expected subject/body and recipient list.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff across local modules/runs: send failure handling -> local persistence -> subsequent-run retry logic. This can be reproduced with a failing email adapter and real local filesystem in-process test harness. Integration-brake: real OS/network semantics are not required to validate this behavior wording; model output quality is irrelevant; no multi-agent sequence. Rejected Unit because behavior spans multiple modules and temporal state across runs. Rejected System because text does not require transport-level fidelity, only fallback and retry orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with email adapter forced to fail; assert report artifact is persisted locally with retry metadata. Simulate next scheduled run (second invocation with same state store) and adapter recovery; assert persisted report is retried and then cleared/marked delivered.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Side-check: no downgrade to Unit, since cross-run persistence/retry is explicit module interaction in behavior text.
